channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dodoma Plans 55.89bn/- Investment for Roads and Bridges in 2026/27
item.description: Dodoma Region in Tanzania is planning to invest 55.89 billion shillings in improving road and bridge infrastructure during the 2026/27 financial year. Out of the total budget, 24.18 billion shillings ...

item.title: Government reduces speed limits on motorways and national highways to save fuel
item.description: The National Highways and Motorway Police (NHMP) has announced reductions in speed limits on Pakistan's motorways and national highways as part of the government’s ongoing austerity drive aimed at con...

item.title: Overview of Antipolo 2nd District Special Congressional Election Following Romeo Acop’s Passing
item.description: The Antipolo 2nd District in Rizal is set for a special election on March 14, 2026, to fill the congressional seat left vacant by the late Romeo Acop. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has declared a spe...

item.title: OGRA increases RLNG prices due to higher import costs and system losses
item.description: Islamabad – The Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority (OGRA) has announced a significant hike of 19-22 per cent in the price of regasified liquefied natural gas (RLNG) for March, affecting consumers of the...

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Could Significantly Affect Small and Medium Enterprises in the Philippines
item.description: Rising fuel prices in the Philippines are expected to put a substantial strain on micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs), which constitute the bulk of the country’s business sector. According to...

item.title: US launches trade investigations on excess capacity and forced labour
item.description: The United States, under President Donald Trump’s administration, has initiated two major trade investigations targeting unfair trade practices and forced labour. The first probe addresses excess indu...

item.title: Impact of Middle East Conflict on Australian Flights to Europe
item.description: Australians planning European travel this northern summer face disruption, rising costs, and safety concerns due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly involving Iran, Israel, and th...

item.title: New Study Explains How Cats Use Flexible Spines to Land on Their Feet
item.description: A recent study published in The Anatomical Record sheds new light on the long-standing 'falling cat problem,' explaining why cats can almost always land on their feet. Led by physiologist Yasuo Higura...

item.title: Neuville Tops Times as 2026 Safari Rally Starts in Naivasha
item.description: The 2026 WRC Safari Rally officially started today morning in Naivasha, with Belgian driver Thierry Neuville setting the fastest time in the shakedown stage. Neuville, making his 185th WRC start and s...

item.title: India Supports UN Security Council Resolution Condemning Iran's Attacks on Gulf Countries and Jordan
item.description: On Thursday, March 11, 2026, India joined nearly 140 member states in supporting United Nations Security Council Resolution 2817 (2026), which was adopted with 13 votes in favour, none against, and ab...

item.title: WHO warns airspace limits delay critical medical aid to over one million people in conflict zones
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don raise alarm say the ongoing US/Israel-Iran war don dey affect emergency medical aid for over 1.5 million people across 25 countries. According to WHO, plenty ur...

item.title: Rising Oil Prices Boost Canadian Energy Sector Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: Recent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil passage, have caused significant volatility in oil markets, briefly pushing Brent crude to US$119.50 per barrel. Although prices have sin...

item.title: House of Reps Investigates $18bn Spent on Refineries Amid NNPC Delay
item.description: Di Chairman of House of Representatives Committee on Petroleum Resources (Downstream), Ikenga Ugochinyere, don talk say di NNPC no dey cooperate, na im dey delay di National Assembly investigation of ...

item.title: Kenya Introduces Bill to Make Matchfixing a Criminal Offence
item.description: Kenya has taken a major step in fighting matchfixing in sports with the presentation of the Sports (Amendment) Bill 2026 in the National Assembly on Wednesday. Nominated legislator Irene Mayaka, who s...

item.title: Tanzanian Businessman Rostam Azizi Acquires Majority Stake in Nation Media Group
item.description: Nation Media Group (NMG), one of Africa’s largest media houses, is getting a new majority owner. Tanzanian businessman Rostam Azizi, through his company Taarifa Ltd, is set to acquire a controlling 54...

item.title: FlySafair introduces temporary fuel surcharge on domestic flights
item.description: FlySafair has announced that it will implement a fuel surcharge on its domestic airline tickets starting Thursday, due to a sudden spike in jet fuel costs. The airline reported that the price of jet f...

item.title: How a Single Debate Changed My Perspective on Climate Change
item.description: Jacob Martin grew up in a small rural town in New Zealand where climate change was largely dismissed or joked about. Initially sceptical, his early understanding of the issue was shaped by local attit...

item.title: FCTA offers free surgeries and support for vulnerable women at Kuje General Hospital
item.description: For International Women's Day 2026, the Federal Capital Territory Administration (FCTA) provide free surgical treatment plus other support to seven indigent mothers for Kuje General Hospital. Dis prog...

item.title: Jaecoo unveils £45,000 seven-seat plug-in hybrid SUV with 700-mile range for UK market
item.description: Jaecoo, the Chinese brand renowned for its rapid growth in Britain, has introduced its new flagship model, the Jaecoo 8 SHS-P, a plug-in hybrid SUV priced from £45,000. This represents the marque's mo...

item.title: SILICE: Spain's Pioneering Open Scientific Information System Based on Non-Commercial Sources
item.description: The article critiques the commercialization of scientific information systems, highlighting how private publishers and databases like Web of Science, Scopus, and Dimensions profit from research produc...
